title: lonchocarpus scorpioides
intro: Lonchocarpus scorpioides, also known as the Coral Tree or Poison Tree, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its seeds, which contain a toxic compound called rotenone, used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The seeds of Lonchocarpus scorpioides have been reported to have several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as decoctions and infusions, are traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, although caution is advised due to the toxic nature of the plant's compounds.
